extra.message: Arbitrary Python code execution via eval(). User input from request.form['expression'] (line 116) is passed directly to eval(expression) on line 120 with no sanitization. An attacker can execute arbitrary Python code such as __import__('os').system('id').

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: OS command injection via unsanitized user input. The address parameter from request.form['address'] (line 136) is concatenated directly into a shell command: rp('nslookup ' + address). The rp function uses os.popen (imported on line 4). An attacker can inject arbitrary commands using shell metacharacters, e.g. 'example.com; cat /etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: XML External Entity (XXE) injection. The XML parser is created with etree.XMLParser(no_network=False, dtd_validation=True) on line 157, explicitly enabling network access and DTD validation. User-supplied XML from request.form['xml'] is parsed on line 159 without disabling entity resolution. An attacker can read local files via <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM 'file:///etc/passwd'> or perform SSRF attacks.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) via Jinja2. User input from request.form['name'] (line 189) is embedded into a template string using Python string formatting (%s) on line 204, then the template is rendered with render_template_string() on line 205. An attacker can inject template expressions like {{config}} or {{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()}} to achieve remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine

extra.message: Hardcoded secret key in source code: app.secret_key = 'Harry Potter And The Deathly Hallows'. This secret key is used for session encryption and should never be hardcoded in the source code, as it can be easily discovered.
Debug mode is enabled in production code (app.debug = True). This exposes sensitive information including stack traces, source code, and the interactive debugger console accessible to attackers.
